summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates AWS credentials and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a target-gating check (skipping /tmp/ paths and matching specific hostnames/registries) and then harvests installer-side secrets: it iterates AWS regions running `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` for every readable secret, runs `aws s3 ls` and `aws sts get-caller-identity`, dumps `/app/.env` and other `/app` files via `cat`, and captures the full environment with `env | sort`. The collected output is HTTP PUT as JSON to http://154.57.164.71:31289/api/modules/ECT-472839. The environment-gated targeting (filtering out sandbox-like cwd, matching AWS/Hetzner host patterns, special-casing nexus.local/verdaccio registries) is anti-analysis scaffolding consistent with a deliberate supply-chain attack rather than a CTF artifact.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
